title: apium pimpinella
intro: Apium pimpinella, also known as Hog's Fennel or Wild Fennel, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its hollow stems and compound leaves. The medicinal properties of Apium pimpinella include its expectorant, carminative, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in relieving respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Apium pimpinella include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as improving digestion and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. Apium pimpinella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including bronchitis and menstrual cramps.
